summary: Call Rwanda Revenue Authority now (Kigali business hours) to secure a 30-minute discovery/demo this week; if no connect, email immediately and schedule next touch; post status within 60 minutes.
reasoning: Most impactful overdue revenue task; sales agent is idle; it is currently within Kigali business hours; prior directives remain unexecuted—this action advances pipeline and clears backlog.

task.title: RRA follow-up: call now and book 30-minute discovery/demo (Kigali/CAT)
task.description: Use the existing RRA follow-up playbook on record and execute now with these checkpoints: 1) Review CRM/account notes; confirm if last follow-up email was sent. If not, prepare it now with three 30-minute slots within the next 3 business days (Kigali/CAT), a clear agenda, one-pager, and relevant case study (cc known stakeholders). 2) Call the primary contact immediately (08:30–17:30 CAT window). If unreachable, leave voicemail (if available) and within 10 minutes attempt an alternate via the RRA switchboard. 3) On live contact, confirm needs, stakeholders, decision process, timeline, and any procurement/compliance requirements; handle objections; aim to book the 30-minute discovery/demo and send a calendar invite before ending the call. 4) Log all call attempts, emails, and outcomes in the CRM; if the meeting is not booked, schedule the next follow-up within 48 hours with blockers noted. 5) If numbers are invalid or email bounces, immediately flag specific gaps for enrichment and hand off to the research agent. 6) Post a brief status update within 60 minutes summarizing attempts, outcomes, and next steps.
